case_title: JEANETTE RAMOS VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOHN STEWART COMPANY, ANA BOLTON, HOTEL ISABEL AND HOTEL GRAND SOUTHERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. THE ISSUE OF PLAINTIFF FAILING TO MAKE RENT PAYMENTS WAS THE CENTRAL ISSUE IN THE PRIOR CASE. AS A CONSEQUENCE, PLAINTIFF NEEDED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE AT THAT TIME AS TO ANY DEFENSE SHE HAD FOR HER FAILURE TO PAY RENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JOHN STEWART COMPANY, ANA BOLTON, HOTEL ISABEL AND HOTEL GRAND SOUTHERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Jonathan Joiner of the Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt (391-6496) for plaintiff and responding party
Jay Brown of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich (925-734-0990) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: sustained with 30 days leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred by collateral estoppel. The issue of plaintiff failing to make rent payments was the central issue in the prior case. As a consequence, plaintiff needed to present evidence at that time as to any defense she had for her failure to pay rent. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/xAM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2008 Continued To Jul-24-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
